factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is stable as it maintains compliance with its debt covenants.

evidence.text: As of February 28, 2019, Cintas was in compliance with all debt covenants. The company's ratings were stable, indicating no significant change in its creditworthiness.

factors.summary: The company is actively returning capital to shareholders through share buybacks, signaling management confidence.

evidence.text: During the nine months ended February 28, 2019, under the new share buyback program, Cintas purchased 0.8 million shares of Cintas common stock... subsequent to February 28, 2019 through April 5, 2019, Cintas has purchased a total of 1.2 million shares of Cintas common stock.

factors.summary: The company does not face material litigation or significant financial or operational impact from legal proceedings.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings... the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect on the consolidated financial position or results of operation of Cintas.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is improving, driven by increased sales volume and customer penetration.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 5.9% for the three months ended February 28, 2019 over the same period in the prior fiscal year, from $1,589.1 million to $1,682.3 million. Revenue increased organically by 6.0% as a result of increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2018

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, supported by increased sales volume and customer activity.

evidence.text: Revenue increased organically by 6.0% as a result of increased sales volume. Organic growth adjusts for the impact of acquisitions and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2018

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting favorably, with higher-margin segments contributing positively.

evidence.text: Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment revenue increased 5.7%... The gross margin was 80 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s third quarter gross margin of 44.1%. The increase was driven by new business sold by sales representatives, penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and continuous improvements in process efficiency.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2018

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to higher sales and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The gross margin was 80 basis points higher than the prior fiscal year’s third quarter gross margin of 44.1%. The increase was driven by new business sold by sales representatives, penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and continuous improvements in process efficiency.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2019 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2018
